commands.review_summary:
This PR provenance-tags OpenAI Responses encrypted reasoning replay, strips unproven or mismatched `encrypted_content`, retries once after `invalid_encrypted_content`, updates agent tests, and adds a changelog note.

Reproducibility: yes. The PR body includes a before Crabbox run reproducing the injected stale `encrypted_content` failure on live `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, and current main source still replays JSON reasoning signatures without provenance; I did not rerun the live repro in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: Human maintainer review is required because the PR has the protected `maintainer` label and the remaining decision is whether to land this focused session-replay behavior and track linked leftovers separately.

target.title: fix(agents): provenance-bound Codex reasoning replay
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex Responses `thinkingSignature` replay can include provider-encrypted `encrypted_content` that is only valid for the context that produced it; mirrored or stale transcripts can replay that blob later and fail the turn with `invalid_encrypted_content`.
- Solution: make encrypted reasoning replay provenance-bound. New Responses reasoning signatures are tagged with internal provider/API/model/baseURL/session provenance; request assembly preserves `encrypted_content` only when that provenance matches the current replay context, and strips unproven or mismatched encrypted blobs before the provider request.
- Fallback: keep the targeted one-shot retry for provider-side `invalid_encrypted_content` so same-session encrypted replay that later becomes invalid can still recover without changing visible transcript/tool replay structure.
- Scope boundary: no Telegram-specific behavior, channel fallback UX, compaction auth selection, or generic transcript rewriting is changed.

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83836
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` turns fail with `400 invalid_encrypted_content` when replay history contains stale mirrored `thinkingSignature.encrypted_content`.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux lease `cbx_b9dbab90e1a8`, run `run_bbfd3e6b74ae`, current branch checkout with dirty fix synced, live `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` through PI runtime using an isolated OAuth profile state.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: full-resync Crabbox script created an isolated OpenClaw state, ran a live Codex turn, mutated the saved assistant thinking signature to contain legacy/no-provenance stale `encrypted_content`, then ran a second live Codex turn with redacted model payload debug. The same script also ran a separate live session that observed a newly tagged encrypted reasoning signature, proved same-session replay preserved `encrypted_content`, corrupted that matching-provenance encrypted blob, and verified the retry fallback recovered.

Evidence after fix: the legacy stale replay path returned `SECOND_ROOT_OK`, printed `ROOT_PROOF_LEGACY_STALE_STRIPPED_BEFORE_REQUEST=true`, did not log `invalid_encrypted_content`, did not log the encrypted-reasoning retry marker, and the redacted request payload did not contain `"encrypted_content"`. The valid replay path printed `VALID_TAGGED_ENCRYPTED_SIGNATURES=1`, `ROOT_PROOF_VALID_SAME_SESSION_REPLAY_PRESERVED=true`, and `ROOT_PROOF_MATCHING_PROVENANCE_PROVIDER_REJECT_RETRY=true`.

Observed result after fix: unproven stale encrypted reasoning is stripped before first request assembly, matching same-session encrypted reasoning remains replayable, and provider-rejected matching encrypted reasoning still recovers via the sanitized retry fallback.

What was not tested: Telegram delivery UX, channel-visible fallback messaging, and the separate compaction/auth-profile concern from the issue thread.

Before evidence (optional but encouraged): AWS Crabbox run `run_ca2af4a2e5ad`, lease `cbx_712f8d43b376`, reproduced the same injected stale encrypted replay with `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`; second turn failed with `code=invalid_encrypted_content` and printed `REPRODUCED_CODEX_INVALID_ENCRYPTED_CONTENT=true`.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `convertResponsesMessages` replayed JSON `thinkingSignature` blocks as Responses `reasoning` input items, including provider-owned `encrypted_content`, without proving that the encrypted blob came from the same provider/API/model/base URL/session context now assembling the request.
- Missing guardrail: prior durable transcripts had no replay provenance marker, so stale mirrored encrypted reasoning looked indistinguishable from valid same-session encrypted replay.
- Fallback rationale: even proven same-session encrypted replay can still be rejected by the provider after server-side expiry/rotation, so `invalid_encrypted_content` remains recoverable by retrying the same request shape with only encrypted blobs removed.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target test file: `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
- Added/updated coverage:
  - stream output tags encrypted Responses reasoning signatures with private replay provenance
  - native Codex replay strips legacy/unproven encrypted reasoning before request assembly
  - matching provenance preserves encrypted reasoning and removes the private marker from provider payloads
  - mismatched provenance strips encrypted reasoning while preserving normal reasoning IDs where applicable
  - retry fallback strips nested `encrypted_content` without changing reasoning/message/function-call IDs or tool pairing

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` (local, post-rebase): 342 tests passed.
- `$autoreview`: `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode auto`; clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- AWS Crabbox live proof: provider `aws`, run `run_bbfd3e6b74ae`, lease `cbx_b9dbab90e1a8`; live stale strip-before-request, live same-session replay preservation, live matching-provenance retry fallback, and focused remote Vitest all passed.
- AWS Crabbox changed gate: provider `aws`, run `run_396dee1c0b0f`, lease `cbx_30249c5e1423`; `pnpm check:changed` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: one additional Responses retry can occur after the provider returns `invalid_encrypted_content`; the retry removes opaque encrypted reasoning blobs and preserves visible replay/tool structure. The new provenance marker stores hashes for base URL/session context rather than raw context values.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
